briefs.title: BC Truckers Can Now Use Approved Traction Devices Instead of Chains on Winter Roads
briefs.summary: BC updated its Motor Vehicle Act rules to let commercial drivers use approved traction devices (grip-enhancing equipment like AutoSocks) instead of chains when chain-up signs are posted, as long as the provincial minister has authorized the specific device and all conditions are met.
briefs.action_required: Check if your traction devices are ministerially authorized and keep written proof of authorization in every vehicle.

key_dates.label: BC traction device chain alternative rule takes effect
